text:
A gentle wind rustled the leaves. Emma approached a clearing. There, a tiny dragon curled up next to a dew-kuckets flower. She smiled and began to slowly approach.

"This flower wouldn't look good with your scales," Emma joked.

The little dragon glared. "Don't mess with us!" it hissed.

Emma chuckled. "Sorry, but you know I'm a friend.” She sat down a respectful distance away. "Do you want some berries?"

The dragon sniffed curiously. "Nah." It huffed dismissively. "Don't waste your time."

Emma laughed again. "Well, have a good one." She stood back up. "I've gotta go."

She paused. "Actually... could I give you a ride?" A quiver of delight appeared in its tiny eye.

Emma grinned. "No problem, little friend. I just might." She gave it a small nuzzle before sitting beside it on the trail. The dragon seemed surprisingly cozy
